algorithms.name: RSA-2048/SHA-256
algorithms.notes: Current RPKI baseline; SPKI size is an approximate DER value. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: ML-DSA-65
algorithms.notes: Primary balanced PQC candidate. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: ML-DSA-87
algorithms.notes: High-assurance candidate with larger objects. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s
algorithms.notes: Hash-based diversity candidate; signature size is the main concern. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: SLH-DSA-SHAKE-192s
algorithms.notes: Conservative diversity candidate with very large signatures. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: P-256/SHA-256
algorithms.notes: Compact classical counterfactual; not the current RFC 6488 RPKI profile. Signature size uses the conservative DER maximum. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: Ed25519
algorithms.notes: Compact classical counterfactual; not the current RFC 6488 RPKI profile. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: ML-DSA-44
algorithms.notes: Measured comparison excluded from the primary profile by the Category 3 policy floor. Timed operations each include one OpenSSL process launch.

algorithms.name: Falcon-512
algorithms.notes: Best size/performance candidate in the 2025 RPKI study; no final RPKI-ready X.509/CMS path. In-process timings are not directly comparable with OpenSSL CLI timings.
algorithms.specification: NIST-selected Falcon; FN-DSA final profile pending

algorithms.name: Falcon-1024
algorithms.notes: Category-5 Falcon comparison. In-process timings are not directly comparable with OpenSSL CLI timings.
algorithms.specification: NIST-selected Falcon; FN-DSA final profile pending

algorithms.name: MAYO-1
algorithms.notes: Multivariate candidate with compact signatures; not standardized. In-process timings are not directly comparable with OpenSSL CLI timings.

algorithms.name: SNOVA-(24,5,4)
algorithms.notes: Multivariate candidate with very compact signatures; not standardized. In-process timings are not directly comparable with OpenSSL CLI timings.

algorithms.name: HAWK-512
algorithms.notes: Promising lattice candidate retained as metadata-only because liboqs 0.15.0 and OpenSSL 3.6.2 do not provide it.
algorithms.reason: candidate is not enabled by oqs-python/liboqs

algorithms.name: RSA-2048+ML-DSA-44
algorithms.notes: Component-size sum excluding composite ASN.1 overhead; no local X.509/CMS interoperability evidence.
algorithms.reason: No local composite signature implementation or interoperability profile

algorithms.name: P-256+ML-DSA-44
algorithms.notes: Component-size sum excluding composite ASN.1 overhead; no local X.509/CMS interoperability evidence.
algorithms.reason: No local composite signature implementation or interoperability profile

algorithms.name: P-256+Falcon-512
algorithms.notes: Component-size sum excluding composite ASN.1 overhead; Falcon/FN-DSA profile is not final.
algorithms.reason: No local composite signature implementation or interoperability profile

complete_object_workload.reason: No existing generator in this environment can produce complete PQC RFC 6488 Manifest payloads and CMS SignedData; projected crypto work is not object-level throughput.

bulk_signing_metadata.key_roll_projection: The synthetic key-roll lower bound uses 10 child certificates, 100 RFC 6488 objects, one manifest, and one CRL: 213 signatures and 102 key generations (one CA and 101 EE keys).
bulk_signing_metadata.manifest_projection: The signing-only 100,000-manifest value uses two signatures per RFC 6488 object (EE certificate plus CMS signature). Where OpenSSL reports keygen throughput, the crypto lower bound also includes 100,000 one-time EE keys. Both exclude ASN.1/CMS construction, hashing, file I/O, RRDP publication, and HSM latency.
bulk_signing_metadata.method: One OpenSSL speed process per algorithm; provider and process startup are outside timed loops.

composite_100k_metadata.classification: sequential component-operation benchmark, not a LAMPS composite encoding
composite_100k_metadata.excluded: composite OID/ASN.1 encoding, domain separation, CMS/X.509, file I/O, HSM latency
composite_100k_metadata.timing_scope: sequential component signing and verification in one process

exact_100k_metadata.excluded: process startup, compilation, key generation, object encoding, file I/O, HSM latency
exact_100k_metadata.key_generation: one key pair before each algorithm's timed loops
exact_100k_metadata.timing_scope: exact EVP sign and verify loops; EVP_MD_CTX initialization is included per operation

migration_scenarios.observation: Legacy validators reject or ignore branch
Algorithm suite mismatch can break path validation
Avoid silent downgrade and split VRP views

object_generation_feasibility.reason: No existing MFT/ROA payload generator was available; ASN.1 payloads were not reimplemented.

primitive_benchmark_metadata.comparability: Compare timings only when comparable_group is identical.
primitive_benchmark_metadata.timing_scope: Core comparison rows use end-to-end OpenSSL CLI wall-clock time.

recommendation.challenger_evidence: Pinned liboqs primitive measurement plus literature and size-model evidence; no X.509/CMS interoperability.

ccr_design.diagnosis: Decode and compare ROAPayloadState.rps when hashes differ.
ccr_design.equality: Compare CCR ROAPayloadState.hash values when CCR output is available.
ccr_design.provenance: Compare trust-anchor/source attribution separately from VRP-set equality.

vrp_equivalence.method: local canonical JSON approximation; not draft-ietf-sidrops-rpki-ccr DER
